reviewer_audit_mode_pivot_quality.reflection: comment-analyzer normal mode (analyze code comments for accuracy/maintainability) mapped naturally to SDTM atomization quality audit via 3-axis analogy: 'comment rot ↔ verbatim drift across rounds' (atoms emitted in round N can become stale relative to evolving PDF understanding in round N+M; structural sweep is the equivalent of doc-rot detection); 'comment factual accuracy vs code ↔ verbatim atom vs PDF ground truth fidelity' (NEW8 substring n-gram canonical-variable check is the equivalent of comment-mentions-deprecated-API check); 'comment-code mismatch (comment says X, code does Y) ↔ atom_type vs PDF semantic content mismatch' (e.g. atom_type=HEADING when PDF actually shows LIST_ITEM = analogous to docstring claim mismatch). pr-family 3rd-agent depth burn observation: comment-analyzer brings detail-oriented per-atom verbatim-ground-truth comparison posture which materialized as careful PDF cross-check WITH inline quoted PDF ground truth in every verdict reason field — adjacent to silent-failure-hunter's skeptical-default posture (round 6 #40) but distinct in style (comment-analyzer is annotator-style with detailed verbatim quotes; silent-failure-hunter is auditor-style with terser failure-mode framings).

findings_added.scope: Multi-session protocol gap: kickoff §3 STEP 0.2 reviewer slot allocation table listed `firecrawl:skill-gen` (slot #42) and `data:debugging-dags` (slot #41 sister batch 32) as reviewer agents. Both are registered SKILLS (invokable via Skill tool) but NOT registered AGENTS (Agent tool subagent_type). Agent tool dispatch fails with 'Agent type not found' error listing all valid agents (40+ available). Sister batch 32 (data family) likely faces same problem.
findings_added.rule_violation: v1.3 prompt + multi-session protocol kickoff template assumes reviewer slot is dispatchable via Agent tool; gap = no validation of skill-vs-agent distinction at kickoff allocation time. CLAUDE.md routing rule + reconciler retro round 6 D-MS-7 family pool pivot recommendations included skill-only families (data + firecrawl) as agent candidates, conflating skill registration with agent registration.
findings_added.repair_action: Main session pivoted #42 to pr-review-toolkit:comment-analyzer (pr-family 3rd-agent intra-family depth burn) as substitute. Decision rationale: (a) preserves batch 34 #43 type-design-analyzer pre-allocation; (b) extends round 6 pr-family 2-burn pattern (#38+#40) to 3-agent depth = consistent intra-family AUDIT recipe consistency validation; (c) zero cross-round Rule D collision maintained (40 unique slots burned cumulative pre-batch-33; #42 unique post-batch-33 = 41 cumulative; sister batch 32 will independently pivot for #41 substitute likely also pr-remaining or other available agent family); (d) per D-MS-3 family-agnostic AUDIT recipe — recipe holds across 22nd AUDIT pivot at pr-comment-analyzer.
findings_added.v1_4_codification_candidate: v1.4 codification: kickoff agent allocation MUST validate skill-vs-agent distinction at pre-dispatch time; document available agent registry (40+ agents) explicitly in kickoff §3 reviewer pool partition; drop skill-only families (data + firecrawl) from D-MS-7 pivot recommendations.
